original_abstract: Attack detection problems in the smart grid are posed as statistical learning problems for different attack scenarios in which the measurements are observed in batch or online settings. In this approach, machine learning algorithms are used to classify measurements as being either secure or attacked. An attack detection framework is provided to exploit any available prior knowledge about the system and surmount constraints arising from the sparse structure of the problem in the proposed approach. Well-known batch and online learning algorithms (supervised and semisupervised) are employed with decision- and feature-level fusion to model the attack detection problem. The relationships between statistical and geometric properties of attack vectors employed in the attack scenarios and learning algorithms are analyzed to detect unobservable attacks using statistical learning methods. The proposed algorithms are examined on various IEEE test systems. Experimental analyses show that machine learning algorithms can detect attacks with performances higher than attack detection algorithms that employ state vector estimation methods in the proposed attack detection framework.
humanized_abstract: Statistical Learning Approach to Optimal Robust Countermeasures of Cyber-Physical System Against Multiple Attacks For different attack scenarios, each with a unique attack vector representing malicious activities behind attacks on power grid, measurements obtained from the batch and online observations are formulated into three classic attack detection problems by the means of statistical learning theory. Based on the mathematical representation of measurement models associated with normal and malicious operating conditions, each of the attack detection problems is transformed into a machine learning classification problem by modeling secure and attack measurements as distinguishable classes. Accordingly, a detection framework has been put forward based on possible prior knowledge of power system and the sparse nature of attack measurements. As part of the solution, classical batch and online learning techniques including supervised and semisupervised learning have been formulated at the decision and feature level fusions in order to tackle the attack detection problem. Additionally, in this research, statistical and geometric properties associated with the attack vectors related to various attack scenarios are matched against the employed learning techniques to exploit the efficiency of statistical learning theory for detecting unobservable attacks in smart grids. Numerical studies carried out on modified IEEE test systems show the superior performance of the learning techniques utilized in this work and, hence, demonstrate that the machine learning algorithms yield more accurate results compared with well-known vector state estimation attack detection methods provided in the literature for the proposed detection framework.

document.input: Attack detection problems in the smart grid are posed as statistical learning problems for different attack scenarios in which the measurements are observed in batch or online settings. An attack detection framework is provided to exploit any available prior knowledge about the system and surmount constraints arising from the sparse structure of the problem in the proposed approach.
